title: Walking Skeleton, Core Behavior, and Preserved Invariants · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for walking skeleton, core behavior, and preserved invariants contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cumulative working product with invariant evidence and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.
Implement one domain operation with explicit preconditions, transition, postconditions, failure taxonomy, and stakeholder-visible result.
Encode critical invariants at the closest layer and prove a deliberate violation is rejected without corrupting prior state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Refactor Evidence evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Refactor Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Refactor Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Refactor Evidence, keep Refactor Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Refactor Evidence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Refactor Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Refactor Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Refactor Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Refactor Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Passing type checks proves a refactor preserved behavior.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the debug Refactor Evidence case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the debug Refactor Evidence case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the debug Refactor Evidence case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the debug Refactor Evidence case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-refactor-evidence-ck9ir1: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Refactor Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Refactor Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Refactor Evidence, keep Refactor Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
pp1-refactor-evidence-ck9ir1:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.
